title: Bounce Back Recycling
description: Bounce Back Recycling is a social enterprise in Ireland that specializes in recycling mattresses and furniture, while creating employment opportunities for the Traveller community.

title: Change Clothes Crumlin
description: Change Clothes Crumlin (CCC) is a community-based clothing reuse hub and registered non-profit organization located in Crumlin, Dublin. Founded in 2022 by Mary Fleming, CCC aims to combat global clothing waste by promoting sustainable fashion practices at the local level. Awardee supported by Rethink Ireland in various impact areas.

title: Educate Together
description: Educate Together is a forward-thinking charity transforming inclusive, learner-centred education in Ireland. It provides equality-based options for thousands of families, ensuring schools are welcoming to all, regardless of social, cultural, or religious backgrounds.  With state-funded, child-centred, and democratically run schools, Educate Together focuses on preparing students for life in the 21st century. As an independent school patron, it collaborates with various supporters and partners to create learning environments that foster equality, respect, and critical thinking.

title: Fighting Words
description: Fighting Words is an Irish non-profit organization dedicated to fostering creative writing skills and a passion for storytelling among individuals of all ages. Founded in 2009 by author Roddy Doyle and former Amnesty International Ireland director Seán Love, the organization offers free tutoring, mentoring, and publishing opportunities to help participants harness the power of their imagination. Awardee supported by Rethink Ireland in various impact areas.

title: iScoil
description: iScoil is a non-profit online learning service in Ireland that provides alternative education pathways for young people aged 13 to 16 who have disengaged from mainstream education. Established to address the educational needs of early school leavers, iScoil offers personalized, flexible, and accredited learning opportunities to help students re-engage with education and achieve recognised qualifications

title: Not So Different
description: Not So Different is a social enterprise dedicated to promoting equality, inclusion, and diversity by supporting creative individuals to fulfil their potential using a personalised development process.

title: The Shona Project
description: The Shona Project is a multi-award-winning social enterprise in Ireland dedicated to educating, empowering, and inspiring young women. It provides workshops, mentorship programs, and resources to help young women build confidence, resilience, and leadership skills. Through initiatives like the SHINE Festival, Survival Handbook, and Junior Ambassador Programme.

title: SpunOut.ie
description: SpunOut.ie is Ireland's youth information website, created by young people, for young people. It aims to empower individuals aged 16 to 25 with information needed to live active, happy, and healthy live.

title: Social Innovation Campus
description: The European Social Innovation Campus (ESIC) is a collaborative initiative focused on upskilling and reskilling the workforce and entrepreneurs in the social economy to address the green and digital transitions. The campus brings together higher education institutions, vocational training providers, social economy organizations, and enterprises from across Europe.

title: Liquid Therapy
description: Liquid Therapy is a multi-award-winning charity based in South Donegal, Ireland, dedicated to providing inclusive and adaptive surf therapy programs. Established in 2011, the organization aims to share the physical and therapeutic benefits of surfing and the ocean with individuals who may not have access to mainstream opportunities. Awardee supported by Rethink Ireland in various impact areas.

title: Trinity Access 21 (TA21)
description: Trinity Access 21 (TA21), an initiative by Trinity College Dublin, aims to transform the Irish education system by fostering college-going cultures and innovative teaching.  Through its Mentoring, Pathways to College, and Leadership in Learning pillars, TA21 provides role models, higher education guidance, and student-led learning to tackle educational disadvantage.  With an extensive outreach programme in post-primary schools, TA21 ensures evidence-based support for students. A longitudinal study tracks engagement and impact, measuring how these initiatives enhance access to higher education.

title: Trinity Centre for People with Intellectual Disabilities
description: The Trinity Centre for People with Intellectual Disabilities (TCPID), situated within the School of Education at Trinity College Dublin, is dedicated to promoting the inclusion of individuals with intellectual disabilities in both education and society. Established in 2004, TCPID offers a comprehensive higher education program designed to enhance students' capacity to participate fully in society as independent adults

title: The Great Care Co-op
description: The Great Care Co-op operates at the Small and Medium-Scale Initiatives (Level 2) impact level. As a start-up with a sustainable business model, it generates employment and addresses community needs through its innovative cooperative structure.

title: FoodCloud
description: FoodCloud is an Irish non-profit social enterprise established in 2013, dedicated to addressing the dual challenges of food waste and food insecurity. By connecting businesses with surplus food to local charities and community groups, FoodCloud ensures that excess food reaches those in need, thereby reducing environmental impact and supporting vulnerable populations.

title: GIY (Grow It Yourself)
description: GIY (Grow It Yourself) is a non-profit social enterprise founded in 2008 in Waterford, Ireland, by Michael Kelly. Its mission is to inspire and enable individuals globally to grow their own food, promoting healthier, more sustainable lifestyles. By 2030, GIY aims to encourage over 100 million people to engage in food growing at home, school, work, and within their communities.

title: Helium Arts
description: Helium Arts is Ireland's national children's arts and health charity, dedicated to enhancing the well-being of children and young people living with lifelong physical health conditions through creative arts. By offering free, multidisciplinary creative workshops in communities, hospitals, and online platforms, Helium Arts provides safe and inclusive spaces where participants can explore their creativity, build friendships, and develop new skills

title: The No Barriers Foundation
description: The No Barriers Foundation is a non-profit organization based in Letterkenny, County Donegal, Ireland, dedicated to removing barriers to exercise and physical activity for individuals with disabilities.

title: Sensational Kids
description: Sensational Kids is an award-winning social enterprise in Ireland dedicated to providing affordable and accessible therapeutic services for children with additional needs. Founded in 2007 by Karen Leigh, the organization addresses the gap between public and private therapy services, ensuring timely support for children facing developmental challenges.

title: Gaisce – The President's Award
description: Gaisce – The President's Award is Ireland's national youth award program, encouraging personal development among young people aged 14 to 25

title: Uplift
description: Uplift is a people-powered campaigning community in Ireland, comprising over 350,000 members who collaborate to drive progressive change through online petitions and coordinated actions

title: Social Ecosystems for a Fair and Inclusive Transition (SEFIT) Fund
Hair Together
description: vocational program combining hairdressing training with mental well-being support.

title: Inclusive Surf Programmes
description: Inclusive surf programmes and practices WEB: https://www.irishsurfing.ie/article/adaptive-surf-instructor-course

title: Innovation in Sport
description: Funding Stream of Sport Ireland

title: Community Services Programme
description: The Community Services Programme supports community-based organisations to provide local social, economic and environmental services that meet identified needs.

title: PEACEPlus Initiative
description: PEACEPLUS is a cross-border funding Programme supported by the European Union, the Government of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland.

title: iSURFATLANTIC
description: Interreg Funding Programme for inclusive tourism development in the Interreg Atlantic Area (AA) is available through the current 2021-2027 program, which supports transnational cooperation projects focused on sustainable tourism and culture.

title: INCLUSEA
description: The INCLUSEA consortium is developing guidelines for inclusive surfing practices through their €3.7 million Erasmus+ Sport project to create an innovative methodology for adapted surf instruction and promote social inclusion for people with disabilities. INCLUSEA project is co-financed by the Erasmus + Sport program of the European Union

title: Connecting for Life - Ireland's national strategy to reduce suicide
description: Connecting for Life is Ireland's national strategy to reduce suicide, implemented by NOSP through coordination of local action plans, public awareness campaigns, and training like the free online “Let’s Talk About Suicide” programme.

title: Area‑Based Childhood Programme (ABC)
description: The ABC and Meitheal model focus on early intervention and prevention, working with families in disadvantaged communities to support child development and address vulnerabilities before crises arise.

title: Meitheal - Prevention, Partnership & Family Support (PPFS)
description: Tusla has developed the Meitheal approach to help children where they may need the support of more than one service. Meitheal is a case co-ordination process for families with additional needs who require multi-agency intervention but who do not meet the threshold for referral to the Social Work Department under Children First. Practitioners in different agencies can use and lead on Meitheal so that they can communicate and work together more effectively to bring together a range of expertise, knowledge and skills to meet the needs of the child and family within their community

title: Housing-led Supports for homeless/ people at risk of homelessness
description: Focus Ireland provides services across Ireland from prevention interventions to securing housing and policy advocacy based on the principle that access to a stable home is central to addressing homelessness and supporting recovery.

title: RTE Toy Show Appeal
description: Strategic grant‑making and philanthropy via the RTE Toy Show Appeal

title: Creative Arts Psychotherapy for vulnerable children - Limerick
description: First Fortnight provides free, accredited arts-based psychotherapy (play, art, music, drama) to children and families in Limerick especially those facing trauma, disadvantage or emotional distress often referred via schools or social services.

title: Journey to Freedom
description: Donegal Domestic Violence Services provides a 24hr helpline and emergency refuge accommodation as well as outreach support for women and children in Donegal

title: Tusla Aftercare Drop-In Services
description: Aftercare: inclusive, responsive support for young people with care experience

title: Crosscare Aftercare Service
description: Aftercare: inclusive, responsive support for young people with care experience

title: 2030 Sustainability Roadmap
description: Integrating sustainable practices aligned with UN SDGs

description: Integrating sustainable practices aligned with UN SDGs

title: Tree planting
description: Integrating sustainable practices aligned with UN SDGs

description: Integrating sustainable practices aligned with UN SDGs

title: Lighthouse Industry Construction Federation Charity - 24/7 Support
description: Free 24/7 Emotional, Physical & Financial Wellbeing Support for Construction Workers & their FamiliesIntegrating sustainable practices aligned with UN SDGs

title: Mental Health & Wellbeing
description: Integrating sustainable practices aligned with UN SDGs

title: Vocational training
description: Skills, Training, Education, person-centred support. Career guidance programme connected to careersportal.ie

title: STEPS+ programme
description: Skills, Training, Education, person-centred support. Career guidance programme connected to careersportal.ie

description: Skills, Training, Education, person-centred support. Career guidance programme connected to careersportal.ie

title: Bord Bia Bloom
description: Skills, Training, Education, person-centred support. Career guidance programme connected to careersportal.ie

title: Addiction Counselling
description: Skills, Training, Education, person-centred support. Career guidance programme connected to careersportal.ie

title: Breaking Down Barriers to Inclusive Care for LGBTQ+ Children & Young People
description: Designed to equip healthcare staff with training and visible allyship tools to improve healthcare access and safety for LGBTQ+ children and adolescents

title: Green Campus & Sustainability
description: Sustainability commitment

title: Innovation Strategy
description: Sustainability commitment

title: Reads & Rhymes Together
description: Sustainability commitment

title: Headstart Fund
description: The HeadStart Fund is a three-year, €3 million fund created by Rethink Ireland in collaboration with the Z Zurich Foundation and the HSE.  The fund will support organisations working with young people aged 15-24 in the Republic of Ireland and will focus on innovative solutions to help groups such as young people, women, and those experiencing socio-economic disadvantage to improve and maintain their mental health and wellbeing over time

title: Breaking Barriers Fund
description: Funded in partnership with the State Street Foundation and the Department of Rural and Community Development through the Dormant Accounts Fund, the Breaking Barriers Fund is a three-year fund combines private philanthropy with public support to drive systemic inclusion. This €1.37 million investment supports people from marginalised communities into sustainable employment, work placements, education, or internships, while also tackling discrimination, prejudice and inequality

title: Innovate Together
description: Innovation fund for social challenges

title: Ktool
description: Advanced digital tool designed to transform the way we tackle the most pressing social problems

title: A Drop in The Ocean [ADITO]
description: ‘A Drop in the Ocean’ programme blends beach workshops with ocean time through activities such as surfing, bodyboarding, and bodysurfing. The project will be delivered in three Donegal locations; Bundoran, Ballyshannon and Donegal town. Designed with psychologists and psychotherapists, the beach workshops aim to build a toolkit of resiliency in each young person, imparting practical advice that supports each individual in their everyday life, whilst facilitating social connection, physical activity and access to nature

title: Big Brother Big Sister
description: Liquid Therapy partners with Irish Gap Year and provides a weekly programme giving each of our individuals a surfing Big Brother or Big Sister. This programme provides social and health benefits for all participants as well a lifelong love of the Ocean and surfing.  Age: ages 6+  Time: up to 2 hours per session  When: Spring & Autumn  Support Level: 1 to 1 support

title: Generation Pride
description: Generation Pride is a wellbeing and resilience programme for LGBTQI+ communities tackling social isolation, anxiety, and depression. It will nurture supportive networks, equip participants with lasting mental health skills, and empower them to address social determinants of mental health. Tailored to vulnerable groups - trans, non-binary, and intersectional groups, it will be culturally competent and inclusive

title: School Workshops
description: Aims to enhance the lives of young LGBTQI+ individuals by sharing personal stories and conducting educational workshops for secondary school students, teachers, parents, and guardian

title: LGBTQI+ Inclusion Training
description: Shoutout offer LGBTQI training

title: Creative Arts Therapy
description: First Fortnight offers creative therapy services, through the modalities of art, drama and music, for young people experiencing or at risk of homelessness. These services are free of charge to support beneficiaries, and for this project, these services will be offered specifically to young people aged 15-24

title: Spunout Academy
description: Spunout Academy is Ireland’s first mental health and emotional wellbeing e-learning platform that will provide a range of free online course curriculums to equip young people aged 16-24 with the tools to improve their emotional literacy, emotional regulation, and ability to engage with pathways to support. Course content will be youth-informed and directed to meet the needs of marginalised groups.

title: CHIPPS
description: CHIPPS is a multidisciplinary mental health service designed with an attachment and developmental trauma focused approach to target the complex mental health challenges experienced by children exposed to early adverse experiences. The main areas of therapeutic provision include Play/Art Therapy, Cognitive Behavioural Therapy, Individual Psychotherapy and direct work with carers and foster carers

title: Teen Parent Support Programme
description: Support for teenage parents, with implications for mental health, education, and family environment.

title: Hair Together Programme
description: The Hair Together programme is an early intervention programme tackling the social determinants of mental ill-health in young people. Over a 10-week programme, young people are supported by industry volunteers to develop practical hairdressing and barbering skills, alongside evidence-based wellbeing strategies from a psychotherapist to support them to manage mental health, social and behavioural issues. Over the course of the programme, young people are challenged to address limiting self-beliefs and develop their skills in teamwork, time management and self-expression, whilst providing them with a career pathway.

title: SPACE
description: The SPACE for Young People project is an early intervention and prevention model working with young people aged 15-24 who have a higher risk of mental illness and social isolation. Operating from the Ballinrobe Family Resource Centre (FRC), SPACE utilises social prescription to provide support on a one-to-one and group basis

title: Meals on wheels
description: Community meal delivery service, social support for vulnerable people.

title: Drug & alcohol support
description: Intervention in addiction and mental health with a rehabilitation component.

title: Counselling
description: Counseling service for emotional well-being, individual/group therapy.

title: Parent toddler groups
description: Early parenting/child support groups, community outreach, and child development.

title: Various groups, camps and workshops
description: Various activities for young people, community building.

title: Pride on the PItch
description: POTP - The Pride on the Pitch project provides a safe and inclusive space for LGBTQ+ young people to participate in physical activity and access mental health resources, and challenges discrimination and harassment in the football community. This project delivers a 20-week football training and coaching programme that functions as a safe space for LGBTQI+ young people aged 15-24 to come together to enjoy the mental health and social benefits that football can bring. ​

title: No Shame
description: The No Shame Mental Health Programme engages members of the Traveller Community to break the shame and stigma of discussing mental health and increase their confidence and emotional resilience. The project has a multi-tiered approach, comprised of diverse, youth-led programmes that are reflective of the different interests of participants and designed to foster social and emotional learning

title: Traveller Voice
description: Focus on specific community (travelers) to give them a voice and encourage participation.

title: Traveller Culture & Identity Programme (TCIP)
description: Cultural identity program for the traveler community.

title: Health and Wellbeing Programme
description: The Health and Wellbeing Programme by Special Olympics Ireland is a comprehensive initiative designed to support the physical, mental, and emotional health of athletes with intellectual disabilities. It includes several key components: 1. Healthy Athletes Programme - a flagship initiative offering free health screenings and education in a fun, supportive environment. It includes seven disciplines.  2. Health Promotion Programme - delivers interactive workshops  such as Healthy eating, Hydration, Personal hygiene and General wellness habits. 3. Healthy Communities - supports local Special Olympics clubs and athletes to become healthier and more active through community-based initiatives.

title: New Trails
description: The New Trails programme is an activity-based intervention service designed to support young people and their families through a combination of outdoor/nature-based adventure activities and Brief Solution Focused Coaching (SFBT). The target group is children and young people, particularly those in foster care placements or facing significant life challenges.

title: Place-based Football Coop groups
description: Football Coop is working with SETU and an advisory board on an implementation plan to identify and establish up to 12 new men's football groups in FAI affiliated clubs around the country by end 2025, and to set up the organisation with professional staff.

title: Drop-In Leagues
description: The Drop-In Leagues programme is designed to increase the number and range of new partnerships with organisations working with people at risk of/experiencing homelessness through establishing new daytime drop-in leagues. Up to 10 partnerships are being established to deliver 10 leagues in 2025.

title: Sanctuary groups nationwide
description: The Sanctuary Runners programme brings together asylum seekers, refugees, migrants, and Irish residents through running, jogging, and walking. Sanctuary Runners has partnerships with national sporting organisations such as Athletics Ireland and Swim Ireland (for Sanctuary Swimmers).

title: Tackle Your Feelings
description: Tackle Your Feelings (TYF) Schools is a free, classroom-based life skills development course. Led by teachers, it enables students to build healthy habits and a practical toolkit for building resilience, emotional intelligence, and positive mental health and wellbeing. Classroom content has been developed using positive and sport psychology principles and is supported by eight comprehensive lesson plans. The journey is enriched by personal insights from some of Ireland’s top rugby players, creating a relatable and inspiring experience for students.

title: Sport & Wellness Fund
description: This two-year Fund offers a package of cash grants and non-financial supports to 5 awardees to help them scale and maximise their impact in promoting healthier lives, increased levels of mental health and wellbeing and improved social cohesion.

title: Brighter Futures Fund
description: The Brighter Futures Fund is a three-year fund aimed at improving mental health and wellbeing for marginalised groups in Ireland. We invite applications from projects and organisations that proactively identify mental health challenges within marginalised communities, enabling early intervention that can prevent the escalation of these issues.

title: Erasmus+ Sport program
description: Erasmus+ is a EU programme for education, training, youth and sport

title: Integrated care programme for children
description: HSE model of care for children with new children's hospital at the centre

title: Entrepreneurship Impact Fund
description: The Entrepreneurship Impact Fund – Strand 1 aims to support mid-stage and mature social innovations to achieve higher levels of impact by scaling their innovations and reaching the next stage of growth.

title: Why Wellbeing Matters
description: In 2024, we completed an academic research project commissioned from the University of Galway evaluating three Rethink Ireland funds. From 2017 to 2023, Rethink Ireland managed these funds to support organisations tackling educational inequality and youth mental health: Education Fund 2017-2020, The Youth Funds 2018-2021 and the Children and Youth Funds 2019-2022. We supported 32 alternative education and youth mental health projects in total across the funds, investing €18 million into the sector

title: Wellbeing Policy Framework of the Department of Education
description: School wellness policy; tools and metrics for schools.

title: Connected Communities Fund
description: The Connected Communities Fund is a three-year fund that aims to build inclusive and resilient communities and strengthen social cohesion across Ireland.  This €1.5 million fund is created by Rethink Ireland in partnership with Mason Hayes & Curran LLP and the Department of Rural and Community Development via the Dormant Accounts Fund.

title: Students' mental health skills CV
description: A co-created initiative to integrate mental health and wellbeing as a fundamental skill in schools. It embeds wellbeing literacy into existing curricula, connects it with employability skills (“skills CVs”), and involves students as peer advocates.

title: Digital wellbeing lab
description: A collaborative space where schools, youth, and tech mentors co-design digital tools and campaigns (apps, TikTok challenges, social media storytelling) that promote healthy online behaviour and mental wellbeing.

title: Community of practice: Teachers as change agents
description: A professional training program for teachers focused on mental health literacy, early identification, and referral pathways. It builds peer learning networks and integrates wellbeing support into continuous professional development.

title: Transition to adulthood pathways
description: A coordinated initiative between the public, private, and community sectors that empowers young people to design their own transition from school to higher education or employment. The prototype seeks to strengthen the interconnections between the education, employment, and mental health systems, ensuring that young people receive holistic support during this critical life stage. The pathways are co-created through a shared learning space involving key actors within Ireland’s mental health ecosystem. Continuous feedback and periodic reviews are embedded using the developmental evaluation approach, allowing the model to adapt and improve over time based on real-time insights.

title: Cross-sector residencies
description: Short-term residencies allowing public servants to work within community mental health projects — and community leaders to spend time inside public institutions — promoting empathy, systemic understanding, and joint problem-solving. This initiative promotes collaboration across local–regional–national levels and rural-urban areas.

title: Adopt a practice
description: A funding (through micro-grants) and mentorship mechanism that enables organisations to adapt proven mental health initiatives from other Irish regions or international contexts, fostering replication and systemic diffusion.

title: National digital infrastructure to measure the true cost of mental health
description: A national digital infrastructure that quantifies the economic and social value of mental health. It demonstrates how wellbeing correlates with productivity, educational attainment, and innovation. This initiative aims to position mental health as a strategic investment, not an expense.

title: National listening infrastructure
description: A continuous “deep listening” system collecting narratives from frontline workers, service users, and communities, combing diverse and interconnected listening channels. Data and narratives are analysed and shared with policymakers in real time to inform decision-making.

title: The living map of the mental health ecosystem in Ireland
description: An open-access, interactive national platform visualising where local mental health initiatives exist and how they influence broader systemic transformation — connecting actors, identifying leverage points, and encouraging collaboration. This platform can be integrated into the national listening infrastructure.
